title: Cryptographic Agility, Dependencies, FIPS, and Policy Evidence · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Algorithm Inventory
summary: A compliance document lists approved algorithms but not the executing module, configuration, boundary, certificate status, dependencies, exceptions, or operating procedures. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the algorithm inventory increment in algorithm inventory and compliance evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Maintain machine-readable use, purpose, owner, suite, key location, dependency, data lifetime, protocol, version, and migration state for cryptographic assets.
Cross-test supported Go versions, architectures, algorithms, parameter sets, external implementations, old data, malformed data, and rollout states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Algorithm Inventory · Theory Crypto Go Algorithm Inventory
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 6239 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind crypto inventory, Go and module versions, policy enforcement, honest compliance status, and expiring exceptions; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain machine-readable use, purpose, owner, suite, key location, dependency, data lifetime, protocol, version, and migration state for cryptographic assets.
